title: Epic 4: Contracts, Workflow Formats, and Inspection
description: Add node contracts, versioned external workflow definitions, parser isolation, and AI-inspectable workflow/contract projections. Source: docs/archetecture/proposal_final.md.

title: contracts-core
description: Add conduit-contract with node contracts, port contracts, schema refs, execution modes, declared capabilities, determinism, retry metadata, and contract validation.
close_reason: Implemented the contracts core crate, added workflow/contract validation, and verified the workspace with fmt, check, test, clippy, and dylint.

title: workflow-format-crate
description: Add conduit-workflow-format with versioned raw workflow definitions that parse into validated domain types without adding parser dependencies to core graph crates.
close_reason: Implemented parser-free conduit-workflow-format crate with versioned raw workflow definitions, typed format errors, conversion into validated workflow domain types, and tests.

title: workflow-format-json
description: Add canonical JSON workflow parsing, round-trip tests, and typed diagnostics for missing or unknown conduit_version.
close_reason: Implemented canonical JSON parsing and serialization for conduit-workflow-format with serde-backed raw structs, typed JSON/format errors, version diagnostics, unknown-field rejection, and round-trip tests.

title: workflow-format-toml
description: Add TOML workflow parsing behind a feature for human-authored local workflow files.

title: workflow-format-yaml
description: Add optional YAML workflow parsing behind a feature, isolated so parser maintenance risk does not affect core crates.

title: introspection-core
description: Add pure workflow/contract introspection data types covering nodes, ports, edge capacities, schemas, capabilities, enforcement level, determinism, and execution mode.

title: introspection-json-snapshots
description: Add JSON rendering for introspection behind serde and snapshot tests for stable AI-facing diagnostics.

title: cli-validate-inspect
description: Add validate and inspect CLI commands once contracts, workflow formats, and introspection data are available.

title: Epic 7: High-Performance Data Tier
description: Defer Arrow/DataFusion until the byte-message runtime and WASM boundary are stable, then add high-performance payload support behind features with benchmarks. Source: docs/archetecture/proposal_final.md.

title: payload-arrow-feature
description: Add Arrow payload support behind an arrow feature after the byte-message runtime and WASM boundary are stable.
close_reason: Added optional Apache Arrow RecordBatch payload tier behind conduit-core arrow feature, with feature tests and WIT boundary rejection for unsupported payloads.

title: bench-backpressure-capacity
description: Add Criterion benchmarks for capacity 1, default capacity, and fan-out/fan-in throughput and latency behavior.

title: api-substrate-leak-check
description: Add a check that public Conduit APIs do not expose asupersync types, preserving the runtime substrate boundary.
close_reason: Added public API substrate leak regression test

title: Epic 1: Conduit Foundation
description: Establish the smallest honest base for Conduit so later work has stable types, validation rules, runtime boundaries, and documentation for foundational decisions. Source draft: docs/epics/epic-1-foundation.md
close_reason: All planned foundation beads cdt-dmh.1 through cdt-dmh.8 are complete and verified; follow-on work has been split into post-foundation cleanup and runtime-bootstrap planning.

title: Workspace skeleton
description: Create the Rust workspace layout, crate boundaries, shared developer entry points, and minimal repo plumbing. Acceptance: workspace builds, crates resolve cleanly, no behavior beyond smoke-level code. Verso target: short architecture note for crate boundaries.
close_reason: Completed before Beads initialization during initial Conduit bootstrap; verified with workspace tests, strict Clippy, and Dylint.

title: Identity primitives
description: Define validated WorkflowId, NodeId, and PortId primitives plus associated parsing, formatting, and generation rules. Acceptance: types are distinct, minimal, invariant-protecting, and tested. Verso target: why each ID exists and what bug it prevents.
close_reason: Completed before Beads initialization as the first foundation type bead; validated opaque IDs with constructors, display/parse support, unit tests, strict Clippy, and Dylint.

title: Workflow model
description: Define workflow definition types, node and edge model, port topology, and graph-level structure. Acceptance: valid workflows are representable, invalid structural graphs are rejected, and model remains separate from runtime behavior. Verso target: graph shape and structural versus semantic constraints.
close_reason: Implemented validated workflow model with node, port, edge, and graph structure.

title: Execution context and message envelope
description: Define runtime-facing context, message/payload envelope, execution metadata, and cancellation/lifecycle hooks at the type boundary. Acceptance: runtime communication boundary is concrete but does not hard-code engine implementation too early. Verso target: why envelope/context fields exist.
close_reason: Implemented runtime context, message envelope, execution metadata, cancellation state, and lifecycle hook boundary types under degraded coordination.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: degraded-coordination: Agent Mail unavailable in this session; intended files: crates/conduit-core/src/lib.rs, crates/conduit-engine/src/lib.rs, crates/conduit-runtime/src/lib.rs, crates/conduit-workflow/src/lib.rs, crates/conduit-cli/src/main.rs

title: Capability and boundary types
description: Define types that express what a node may receive, emit, or do, including capability descriptors and permission-like boundaries. Acceptance: capability rules are explicit, separate from workflow structure, and invalid combinations are rejected. Verso target: runtime safety and isolation tradeoffs.
close_reason: Implemented explicit node capability descriptors for port and runtime effect boundaries with validation for duplicate and conflicting claims.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: degraded-coordination: Agent Mail unavailable in this session; intended files: crates/conduit-core/src/lib.rs, crates/conduit-core/src/capability.rs, crates/conduit-core/src/context.rs, crates/conduit-core/src/message.rs, crates/conduit-engine/src/lib.rs, crates/conduit-runtime/src/lib.rs

title: Error model
description: Define shared error enums, error codes, user-facing versus internal classification, and recovery or retry categories if needed. Acceptance: errors are structured, stable enough for downstream use, and deterministically mapped. Verso target: error taxonomy and tradeoffs.
close_reason: Replaced the shared stringly-typed runtime result with a structured ConduitError taxonomy carrying stable codes, visibility, retry guidance, and typed conversions for validation failures.

title: Test kit
description: Add reusable builders, fixtures, fake nodes or test doubles, property-test helpers, and reusable invariants. Acceptance: later beads can test model and runtime behavior without repetition and helpers reduce test friction. Verso target: only non-obvious testing strategy.
close_reason: Added a dedicated conduit-test-kit crate with reusable builders and executor doubles, plus property tests for identifier and workflow invariants and improved engine error-path coverage.

title: Documentation pass
description: Add Verso annotations to decision-heavy foundation modules, ensure narrative matches code, and capture rationale while decisions are fresh. Acceptance: the why is close to code for all foundational decisions and docs do not drift from implementation.
close_reason: Added decision-focused module docs for workflow structure, runtime context, lifecycle hooks, capability boundaries, and error taxonomy, and verified the workspace gates remained green.

title: Epic 6: WASM Vertical Slice
description: Prove the extension boundary with a runtime-neutral batch trait, Wasmtime host adapter, strict WASM capability enforcement, and a native plus WASM example workflow. Source: docs/archetecture/proposal_final.md.
close_reason: Completed WASM vertical slice beads through batch trait, Wasmtime adapter, capability enforcement, and mixed native/batch example.

title: wasm-batch-trait
description: Add runtime-neutral BatchExecutor, BatchInputs, and BatchOutputs so the WASM boundary is defined before engine-specific integration.
close_reason: Added runtime-neutral batch executor boundary and WasmModule adapter

title: wasm-wasmtime-adapter
description: Add conduit-wasm with Wasmtime host-owned batch execution: host reads inputs, invokes guest component, validates outputs, and sends via PortsOut.
close_reason: Implemented conduit-wasm Wasmtime Component Model batch adapter with WIT ABI, dynamic Val invocation, conversion tests, and workspace validation.

title: wasm-capability-enforcement
description: Enforce declared capabilities for WASM nodes, keeping native capabilities advisory and WASM/process capabilities strict.
close_reason: Implemented strict capability enforcement for WASM/process contracts and import-free conduit-wasm capability validation while preserving native advisory effects.

title: wasm-native-mixed-example
description: Add a native plus WASM linear pipeline example matching the MVP slice from the architecture proposal.
close_reason: Added checked conduit-wasm mixed native/batch pipeline example and docs, proving host-owned batch boundary through conduit-engine without requiring a guest artifact.

title: Repo metadata and audit scope cleanup
description: Add the missing repo metadata files and refresh the auditor onboarding scope to match the actual workspace layout. Acceptance: root README.md explains what Conduit is and how to build/test it; root LICENSE matches the MIT declaration in Cargo.toml; docs/audits/Audit_scope.md references docs/ paths, workspace crates, and beads tracking rather than stale Documents/ and task_database.md paths.
close_reason: Added root README.md and LICENSE, rewrote docs/audits/Audit_scope.md for the actual workspace and Beads workflow, and verified formatting/check gates remained clean.

title: NodeExecutor contract alignment
description: Reconcile the scaffolded NodeExecutor trait with the proposal before the runtime grows further. Acceptance: code and docs agree on the intended contract; either the proposal is updated to the current sync scaffold or the scaffold is moved toward the intended async/ports shape with explicit placeholder types; downstream crates compile without ambiguity about the public execution boundary. Verso target: why the chosen contract is the right staging point before asupersync integration.
close_reason: Aligned NodeExecutor with the proposal by introducing an async boundary, placeholder port handles, and matching executor tests/docs.

title: Capability and workflow cross-validation
description: Connect node capability claims to the declared workflow topology so the capability layer stops being cosmetic. Acceptance: there is a validation entry point that accepts a WorkflowDefinition plus NodeCapabilities descriptors and rejects mismatched node IDs, unknown ports, and direction disagreements; tests cover node mismatch, port mismatch, direction mismatch, and happy path.
close_reason: Added workflow-capability cross-validation so node capability claims are checked against declared workflow nodes and port directions, with mismatch tests covering node, port, and direction failures.

title: Epic 5: Metadata Productization
description: Make runtime execution explainable through richer typed metadata, message boundary observations, JSONL run logs, tiered metadata policy, and tracing integration. Source: docs/archetecture/proposal_final.md.

title: metadata-message-boundary
description: Emit message metadata from port send and receive boundaries so lineage is captured by the runtime instead of by node implementations.
close_reason: Added message-boundary metadata recording at port send/receive edges, updated runtime port attachment, and verified the workspace with fmt, check, test, clippy, and dylint.

title: metadata-jsonl-sink
description: Add a reproducible JSONL metadata sink for run logs that can support diagnostics and replay-style analysis.

title: metadata-tiered-sink
description: Add a tiered metadata policy adapter that records control facts while avoiding large payload bytes and high-cost data-tier contents.

title: payload-bytes-tier
description: Introduce PacketPayload::Bytes backed by bytes::Bytes for cheap clone/slice behavior in ordinary packet flow.
close_reason: Introduced PacketPayload::Bytes backed by bytes::Bytes

title: payload-control-tier
description: Introduce PacketPayload::Control backed by serde_json::Value for control-plane messages and AI/tool orchestration.
close_reason: Introduced PacketPayload::Control backed by serde_json::Value

title: runtime-tracing-feature
description: Add an optional tracing feature that bridges lifecycle and cancellation observations into tracing without replacing typed metadata.

title: cli-run-explain
description: Add run and explain CLI commands once workflow formats and metadata sinks exist.

title: bench-metadata-overhead
description: Benchmark NoopMetadataSink versus default/tiered sinks to guard the zero-cost-abstraction goal.

title: Epic 2: Runtime Bootstrap
description: Move Conduit from a type-complete scaffold into an honest runtime skeleton with observable execution, an explicit concurrency substrate, and the first real runtime supervision semantics.
close_reason: All Runtime Bootstrap child beads are closed.

title: Runtime lifecycle observation seam
description: Make conduit-runtime responsible for emitting lifecycle events through LifecycleHook so the observability seam becomes real before deeper runtime work lands. Acceptance: run_node dispatches NodeStarted before execution and NodeCompleted or NodeFailed after execution; a no-op default hook keeps existing callers simple; tests verify event ordering and that executor failures still propagate unchanged.
close_reason: Wired lifecycle observation through conduit-runtime with a no-op default hook, explicit hook-aware execution entry point, and runtime tests for started/completed/failed ordering and error propagation.

title: Asupersync runtime skeleton
description: Introduce asupersync as the runtime substrate in conduit-runtime and prove the first structured-concurrency slice against the current scaffold. Acceptance: asupersync is a declared dependency with the proposal/docs linked to the upstream repository; conduit-runtime owns a basic task-tree-backed execution entry point; cancellation and failure handling are expressed in code and tests at the level supported by the current node interface; the runtime remains intentionally narrow and does not yet claim full FBP semantics.
close_reason: Added asupersync as the runtime substrate, documented the upstream source in the proposal, and introduced a narrow asupersync-backed node execution wrapper with cancellation and failure tests.

title: Document Asupersync integration boundary
description: Capture the integration rule that Asupersync stays below Conduit-owned FBP APIs. Conduit should expose NodeContext, PortsIn, PortsOut, metadata, and capability surfaces rather than leaking Asupersync Cx or raw runtime primitives into public node contracts. Acceptance: docs or Verso note names the boundary, explains why Cx remains internal, and identifies where adapters are allowed.
close_reason: Documented Asupersync integration boundary: Conduit owns FBP public APIs and Asupersync remains an internal runtime adapter substrate.

comments.text: degraded-coordination: Agent Mail unavailable; files: docs/conduit_proposal.md, docs/handoff_2026-04-24_late.md, crates/conduit-runtime/src/lib.rs, crates/conduit-core/src/ports.rs

title: Back PortsIn and PortsOut with bounded async channels
description: Replace placeholder port identity handles with Conduit-owned wrappers over bounded async channel endpoints, likely backed by Asupersync mpsc. Acceptance: engine wires declared workflow ports into bounded edge channels; nodes still depend on PortsIn and PortsOut rather than raw Asupersync types; tests prove bounded capacity and basic upstream pressure behavior.
close_reason: Implemented Conduit-owned bounded port handles backed by Asupersync mpsc and wired workflow edges through PortsIn/PortsOut.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: degraded-coordination: Agent Mail unavailable; files: crates/conduit-core/src/ports.rs, crates/conduit-engine/src/lib.rs, crates/conduit-test-kit/src/lib.rs, crates/conduit-core/Cargo.toml, crates/conduit-test-kit/Cargo.toml

title: Expose cancel-safe reserve commit output sends
description: Shape the output-port API around Asupersync's two-phase reserve/commit semantics instead of a naive send().await surface. Acceptance: PortsOut exposes a Conduit-owned reserve/send-permit flow; dropping an uncommitted permit aborts cleanly; tests cover cancellation between reserve and commit with no lost or ghost messages.
close_reason: Added Conduit PortSendPermit reserve/commit flow over Asupersync send permits; dropping or aborting reserved output capacity releases slots without messages.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: degraded-coordination: Agent Mail unavailable; files: crates/conduit-core/src/ports.rs, crates/conduit-core/src/lib.rs

title: Map Asupersync runtime and channel errors into ConduitError
description: Add explicit conversions or adapter-level mapping from Asupersync JoinError, mpsc SendError, and RecvError into ConduitError categories. Acceptance: cancellation maps to cancellation, disconnected edges get a distinct execution/runtime error message, full edge/try-send conditions are represented deterministically, panics are preserved as internal execution failures, and unit tests cover each mapping.
close_reason: Mapped Asupersync runtime/channel errors and Conduit port errors into the shared ConduitError taxonomy with tests.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: degraded-coordination: Agent Mail unavailable; files: crates/conduit-core/src/error.rs, crates/conduit-core/src/lib.rs, crates/conduit-core/src/ports.rs

title: Bridge NodeContext cancellation to active runtime cancellation
description: Move NodeContext cancellation beyond passive state by backing it with a Conduit-owned cancellation view that can be driven by Asupersync task context or handles. Acceptance: pre-cancelled contexts still reject execution; runtime-triggered cancellation is visible inside a running node; child node tasks are cancelled deterministically; public APIs do not expose Asupersync Cx directly.
close_reason: Added Conduit-owned cancellation handles/tokens, wired runtime execution through shared cancellation, and tested active cancellation visibility plus shared child-context rejection.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Coordination note: Agent Mail is unavailable in this environment. I am working in JJ change qllqnnpn on NodeContext/runtime cancellation bridging. Expected files: crates/conduit-core/src/context.rs, crates/conduit-runtime/src/lib.rs, maybe tests/docs.

title: Use deterministic Asupersync runtime config in tests
description: Switch runtime behavior tests that care about ordering, cancellation, or failure propagation to a deterministic current-thread Asupersync runtime configuration. Acceptance: test helpers expose a deterministic runtime constructor; existing runtime tests use it where scheduling determinism matters; production builder defaults remain separate.
close_reason: Added deterministic current-thread Asupersync runtime construction for tests and moved runtime behavior tests that assert ordering, cancellation, or failure propagation onto that helper while preserving production defaults.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Coordination note: Agent Mail is unavailable in this environment. I am working in JJ change kwrwnxym on deterministic Asupersync runtime testing. Expected file: crates/conduit-runtime/src/lib.rs, maybe test-kit helpers if needed.

title: Epic 3: True FBP Core
description: Move Conduit from the sequential workflow scaffold to a true long-lived FBP process graph: async ports, concurrent supervision, edge capacity, cycle policy, and robust runtime tests. Source: docs/archetecture/proposal_final.md.

title: ports-async-recv-send
description: Add async recv, reserve, and send methods on PortsIn/PortsOut with cancellation mapping, while preserving try_* methods for deterministic tests.
close_reason: Implemented async PortsIn::recv plus PortsOut::reserve/send with cancellation-aware port errors and tests. Verified cargo fmt --all --check, cargo test -p conduit-core, cargo check --workspace --all-targets, cargo test --workspace, cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -W clippy::pedantic -W clippy::nursery, and nix develop . --command cargo-dylint-nightly --all.

title: cli-use-conduit-runtime
description: Replace the temporary CLI futures::executor execution path with AsupersyncRuntime so the CLI follows the same runtime boundary as production execution.
close_reason: CLI now uses AsupersyncRuntime for the scaffold entrypoint

title: ports-recv-any
description: Add recv_any for multi-input nodes, stream joins, watcher control inputs, and non-busy polling across declared input ports.
close_reason: Implemented PortsIn::recv_any for multi-input async receive, including cancellation, closed-input handling, and tests. Verified with cargo fmt --all --check, cargo check --workspace --all-targets, cargo test --workspace, cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -W clippy::pedantic -W clippy::nursery, and nix develop . --command cargo-dylint-nightly --all.

title: engine-concurrent-supervisor
description: Replace the primary sequential workflow runner with supervised concurrent node execution: wire all edges first, spawn one task per node, and aggregate terminal results.
close_reason: Implemented concurrent workflow supervision by wiring all ports first, polling every node future concurrently, and aggregating terminal results in workflow order while preserving the borrowed NodeExecutor API. Added tests for bounded capacity-1 source/sink flow and all-node polling before terminal failure reporting. Verified with cargo fmt --all --check, cargo check --workspace --all-targets, cargo test --workspace, cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -W clippy::pedantic -W clippy::nursery, and nix develop . --command cargo-dylint-nightly --all.

title: engine-fail-fast-cancellation
description: Attach one workflow cancellation handle to all node contexts and cancel sibling tasks on first node failure for the MVP failure policy.
close_reason: Attached one workflow cancellation handle to all node contexts and changed the supervisor to request cancellation on the first node failure while draining remaining terminal results. Added a sibling-cancellation regression test. Verified with cargo fmt --all --check, cargo check --workspace --all-targets, cargo test --workspace, cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -W clippy::pedantic -W clippy::nursery, and nix develop . --command cargo-dylint-nightly --all.

title: workflow-edge-capacity
description: Add EdgeCapacity to EdgeDefinition, resolve Default to the engine default capacity, and wire explicit capacities into bounded edge channel construction.
close_reason: Added EdgeCapacity to workflow edges with default and explicit bounded variants, resolved default capacities at the engine boundary, and exposed an explicit-capacity builder for tests. Verified with cargo fmt --all --check, cargo check --workspace --all-targets, cargo test --workspace, cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -W clippy::pedantic -W clippy::nursery, and nix develop . --command cargo-dylint-nightly --all.

title: workflow-topology-diagnostics
description: Add topological ordering and CycleDetected diagnostics using a small hand-rolled graph algorithm before introducing any graph crate dependency.
close_reason: Workflow topology diagnostics implemented and validated

title: workflow-cycle-policy
description: Reject cycles by default and add an explicit cycles-allowed construction path for future feedback-loop workflows.

title: types-identifier-length-cap
description: Add a maximum identifier length and typed TooLong error to conduit-types to close the robustness gap called out in the final proposal.
close_reason: Identifier length cap added to conduit-types

title: engine-backpressure-tests
description: Add deterministic runtime tests proving bounded propagation, blocked sends, downstream receive unblocking, fan-out, fan-in, and closure propagation.
